executiveStatus.rationale: Three high-confidence high-severity defects affect complete text-block processing, dynamic typography protection and release-manifest integrity. A real Qwen 15.8.0 capture is healthy for its observed page, but it does not exercise the two runtime boundary defects.

shareableSourceHygiene.note: Font manifests and OFL license texts are present; binary fonts are intentionally absent from the shareable source tree.

contractMatrix.tests: direction/alignment v15.8 unit tests and browser smoke
contractMatrix.audit: No confirmed boundary regression; standard suite and smoke passed
Bounded continuation passes; cache dependency defect is RTLX-CACHE-001
Static protections exist, but dynamic invalidation fails
Source/test confirmed; real Claude composer remains insufficient_evidence

contractMatrix.tests: runtime snapshot tests; real Qwen report
contractMatrix.audit: Typography pending state works; text-block cap can produce false-complete subset
Current Qwen mismatch selection was cleared rather than reused; no stale projection reproduced
Confirmed privacy flags false and no page text/full URL/form/cookie/storage/network/screenshot payload

contractMatrix.tests: hash recomputation; artifact compare; integrity verifier
contractMatrix.audit: Canonical report and browser ZIPs pass; aggregate release manifest is stale

commands.detail: 468 packages installed; npm reported one high-severity dev dependency vulnerability.
Prettier check passed.
tsc --noEmit passed.
0 errors; 63 warnings.
63/63 warnings present in the reviewed baseline; no stale or unreviewed entries.
11 schemas validated.
12 bundled profiles, certification index and malformed fixtures passed.
90 test files and 249 tests passed; statements 85.87%, branches 75.09%, functions 90.72%, lines 86.95%.
Callback and Promise adapter contract shapes passed; script explicitly does not prove real-browser behavior.
Schemas, profiles, package manifests, fonts and generated manifests validated; four browser builds completed.
Controlled Chromium smoke passed, queues drained, typography verification failures were zero, rollback removed owned mutations.
Generated browser manifests validated.
Firefox: 0 errors/1 warning; Firefox Android: 0 errors/0 warnings.
Repository security scan passed.
No production dependency vulnerabilities.
Readiness-only check passed; no upload or credential use.
Exact release artifact identified, but unpacked extensions are disabled by browser administrator policy.
Edge executable unavailable.
Firefox executable unavailable.
Four audit-only tests reproduced the text-block cap, falsified Latin alternate-discovery rescue, and reproduced both protection-cache transition failures.
The command uses audit-level=critical; npm reported one high-severity undici vulnerability but returned zero.
Personal install identity verified.
dist/evidence/release-attestation.json was absent because attestation generation was not performed in this audit.
RTLX_UNSIGNED_ARTIFACT and RTLX_SIGNED_ARTIFACT were not supplied.
Script emitted status=passed while writing five status=not_run reports.
Aggregate release gates correctly remained blocked because external and signed-artifact gates were not passed.
Static packaging passed; real device/emulator execution not supplied.
User-supplied Qwen 15.8.0 report passed failure-evidence schema 1.2.0 and canonical SHA-256 recomputation.
Outer manifest and ZIP safety passed, but three records in the top release SHA manifest did not match delivered files.
Current build output and all four release ZIPs matched exactly: 56/56 files each, no path or content differences.
One high-severity, fix-available transitive dev dependency vulnerability in undici 7.27.2.

findings.title: Typography Text-node fingerprints omit protection and DOM-state dependencies, producing stale eligibility decisions

evidence.detail: The cache comparison happens before typographySkipReason is reevaluated; fingerprints are stored for protected and eligible nodes alike.
Protection depends on code/icon/layout state and element/ancestor selectors, but the fingerprint contains only text, parent tag, role, dir and contextKey.
class, role, contenteditable, hidden and inert mutations are observed, but mutation handling does not invalidate the per-Text typography fingerprint for those dependency changes.
The cache is reset on settings changes/teardown, not on ordinary protection-class or ancestor-state mutations; batch fingerprints are persisted after each scan.
Both transitions reproduced: protected→eligible remained skipped with inspectedNodes=0, and eligible→protected bypassed protection reevaluation with inspectedNodes=0.

impact: Typography may fail to apply after a protected region becomes normal content.
An already-processed node that becomes code/editor/icon/layout-protected can retain or reuse stale typography state instead of honoring the current hard/protective boundary.
Dynamic React/Vue class and ancestor changes are specifically exposed because Text node identity can remain stable while eligibility changes.
reproduction: Process a Persian Text node under .profile-protected and persist fingerprints; remove the class and rescan with the same context.
Reverse the transition by adding the protection class after the eligible scan.
requiredFix: Make the cache key cover every dependency used by typographySkipReason, including relevant ancestor/protection state, or explicitly invalidate affected Text nodes on matching mutations.
Reconcile already-owned typography mutations when a target transitions into a protected state; skipping future planning alone is insufficient.
requiredTests: Protected→eligible and eligible→protected transitions on the element and an ancestor.
contenteditable, role=textbox, code/math/editor/terminal/icon/layout-sensitive transition fixtures.
Stable Text-node identity under class replacement and framework-style rerender fixtures.
Verification that stale TYPOGRAPHY_CLASS ownership is removed or rolled back when protection becomes active.

findings.title: Top-level release SHA-256 manifest is stale for three delivered artifacts

evidence.detail: The records for RTLX-v15.8.0-COMPONENT-SHA256-MANIFEST.json, RTLX-v15.8.0-EVIDENCE.zip and RTLX-v15.8.0-NEXT-DELIVERY.zip match no delivered candidate by both bytes and SHA-256.
Deterministic verifier exited 1 and printed all three expected/actual size and hash differences.
The outer handoff manifest is internally valid: 542/542 listed non-manifest files matched with exact coverage.
The actual nested component manifest validates all 12 components, limiting the defect to stale top-level release metadata rather than corrupted browser ZIP contents.

impact: Consumers cannot authenticate the complete delivered release set using the advertised top-level manifest.
Automation may reject valid components or trust hashes for artifacts that are not present.
The release cannot be certified as a coherent immutable bundle until the manifest is regenerated and reverified.
reproduction: Run CMD-029 against the delivered handoff.
Compare expected and actual bytes/SHA-256 for the three unmatched names.
requiredFix: Generate the top-level release manifest only after every component and container ZIP is finalized.
Add a release gate that resolves every manifest record to exactly one delivered file and fails on extra, missing, size-mismatched or hash-mismatched artifacts.
Regenerate the outer handoff manifest after correcting release artifacts.
requiredTests: A clean-room release assembly test validating all top-level and nested manifests.
A mutation test proving a one-byte change fails the release gate.
A deterministic rebuild test proving repeated packaging produces identical component and container hashes.

findings.title: Ordered text-block enumeration silently stops after 512 inspected descendants and has no continuation contract

evidence.detail: ENUMERATION_LIMIT is 512; traversal exits when inspected reaches the cap and returns only the selected array, without hasMore/cursor/continuation metadata.
FrameRuntime records discovered and processed counters only from the truncated array and marks the semantic region expanded; no enumeration continuation is queued.
The text-walker rescue path creates candidates only for Arabic-script text, so Latin blocks omitted after the cap are not universally rediscovered.

Audit-only 600-paragraph fixtures returned exactly paragraph-0..paragraph-511; paragraph-599 was absent. An exhausted CandidateDiscoveryCursor discovered only the containing main element for 600 Latin paragraphs.
Conflicts with complete deterministic text-block decomposition and reliable discovered-versus-processed evidence.

impact: Structured regions with more than 512 inspected descendants can leave later natural-language blocks unprocessed.
For Latin-only or Latin-tail blocks, another discovery path does not guarantee recovery.
textBlocksDiscovered == textBlocksProcessed can still be true for the truncated subset, creating a false-complete coverage signal.
reproduction: Construct a main/section with 600 Latin p elements.
Exhaust CandidateDiscoveryCursor and call enumerateTextBlocks; only the container candidate and first 512 blocks are represented.
requiredFix: Replace the one-shot cap with a deterministic resumable enumeration cursor or an equivalent bounded continuation mechanism.
Do not mark a semantic region fully expanded or complete until enumeration has no remaining descendants.
Expose incomplete enumeration in runtime evidence or ensure coverage counters cannot reach a complete state while enumeration remains pending.
requiredTests: A >512 Latin-block fixture that reaches the final block.
A mixed Arabic/Latin fixture proving both discovery paths converge without duplicates.
A quiescence assertion that remains incomplete until text-block enumeration drains.
Boundary tests at 511, 512, 513 and large nested list/table regions.

findings.title: The locked development toolchain contains a fix-available high-severity undici vulnerability

evidence.detail: npm audit --json exited 1 and reported undici 7.27.2 as high severity with fixAvailable=true.
Dependency chain is web-ext 10.4.0 → addons-linter 10.7.0 → cheerio 1.2.0 → undici 7.27.2.
npm audit --omit=dev --audit-level=high found zero vulnerabilities; the packaged extension runtime is not shown to contain this dependency.
audit:all uses audit-level=critical, so it printed the high vulnerability but returned exit 0.

impact: Risk is limited to development/lint/build tooling based on the current dependency classification, not the extension's production dependency graph.
Build agents using affected proxy/cache paths may be exposed to the reported advisory classes.
The current audit:all name can be misread as gating all severities although high findings do not fail it.
reproduction: Run npm explain undici to confirm the transitive chain.
requiredFix: Update the lockfile/toolchain to a dependency graph containing undici >=7.28.0 or an upstream release that removes the vulnerable range.
Change the all-dependency audit gate to fail at the project's accepted severity threshold, or document an explicit time-bounded exception.
requiredTests: npm ci followed by npm audit --json with zero high/critical vulnerabilities.
web-ext lint and all release checks after the dependency update.

findings.title: external-gate status command reports passed while every generated external gate is not_run

evidence.detail: The script creates five reports with status=not_run and evidenceState=insufficient_evidence.
The same invocation prints top-level status=passed unconditionally and exits zero.
CMD-025 reproduced the contradictory successful output.
The authoritative aggregate gate script correctly normalized the same reports to blocked and exited 2.

impact: CI or a human consuming only npm run evidence:external-status can mistake placeholder creation for external validation success.
The output conflicts with CURRENT-STATUS.json and the explicit external-evidence policy.
reproduction: Inspect the five written dist/evidence reports and compare their not_run values with the printed passed value.
requiredFix: Return blocked or insufficient_evidence when any generated report is not_run.
Use a non-zero blocked exit code consistent with release-evidence-gates.mjs.
Separate a 'placeholder_files_created' operation result from evidence gate status.
requiredTests: Unit test all combinations: all passed, any failed, any not_run, any insufficient_evidence and missing report.
CLI exit-code assertions and schema validation for generated status output.

findings.title: Exact release-artifact execution is not established across supported browsers

evidence.detail: Chromium was blocked by administrator policy; Edge and Firefox executables were unavailable; Firefox Android had no device/emulator execution.

impact: Cross-browser API, manifest loading, permission and rollback behavior remain unverified on the exact delivered ZIPs.
requiredTests: Run each exact artifact in an unmanaged clean browser profile and Firefox Android device/emulator; retain machine-readable reports, browser versions and artifact SHA-256.

findings.title: Real Claude composer, IME, keyboard/caret and manual accessibility behavior remains unverified

evidence.detail: status=not_run; requires keyboard, screen-reader, zoom, forced-colors and focus verification.
Editor rules preserve direction, alignment and typography in source, but no real composer fixture was supplied.

impact: Cannot claim real editor preservation, IME safety, caret stability or accessibility certification.
requiredTests: Execute a real Claude composer matrix with Persian/English IME, selection, keyboard navigation, screen reader, zoom and forced-colors evidence.

findings.title: Signed artifact, store validation, installed-update rollback and staged rollout gates remain unverified

evidence.detail: Signed artifacts were not supplied and aggregate release gates remained blocked.
The relevant reports are not_run or insufficient_evidence.

impact: Store-ready, signed, update-safe or rollout-ready claims are not established.
requiredTests: Supply signed/unsigned artifacts, authenticated store validation results, installed 15.7.3→15.8.x update/rollback evidence and staged rollout rehearsal logs.

findings.title: The supplied Qwen 15.8.0 capture is not a controlled same-page regression comparison

evidence.detail: Archive Qwen runtime evidence is 15.7.2/15.7.3, while the user-supplied report is 15.8.0 at a later capture time.
Current selectedElement.status=no_data with RTLX-FEC-SELECTION-LOCATION-MISMATCH-CLEARED, so no selected-block before/after projection is available.

impact: The evidence supports a current 15.8.0 run, but cannot prove that every historical visual inconsistency was fixed on the identical DOM/conversation.
requiredTests: Capture the same saved conversation/DOM fixture under both versions with identical viewport, browser settings, selected blocks and pixel/per-block assertions.

findings.title: Long-run memory retention and framework node-replacement behavior remains unverified

evidence.detail: Committed journal entries intentionally retain strong Node references until rollback/removal.
status=not_run; no eight-hour real-browser memory profile is present.

impact: No evidence establishes a leak, but the requested React/Vue replacement and long-run retention claim cannot be certified from unit tests alone.
requiredTests: Run multi-tab framework replacement and detach/reinsert campaigns with heap snapshots, journal-size trends, ownership counters and rollback assertions.

findings.title: Current source build output matches all four supplied browser release ZIPs exactly

evidence.detail: For chromium, edge, firefox and firefox-android, builtFiles=56 and artifactFiles=56 with no onlyBuilt, onlyArtifact or different entries.
Packaging sorts paths and assigns the fixed 1980 ZIP timestamp.
ZIP CRC checks passed with no unsafe path, encrypted entry or symlink entry.

impact: The browser ZIP contents are traceable to the provided current source build despite the stale aggregate release manifest.
This is content equivalence, not real-browser execution certification.
requiredTests: Keep this comparison as a mandatory clean-source release gate.

findings.title: A real Qwen/Edge RTLX 15.8.0 report is schema-valid, hash-valid and quiescent for the captured page

evidence.detail: productVersion, extensionVersion and processorVersion are 15.8.0; browser is Edge 149 on Windows x86-64 with official:qwen profile v3.
Schema 1.2.0 validation passed and recomputed canonical report hash exactly matched sha256:0ab887e93bb34e2771a8468a2707adcb0b8cccc78eedbbe2d8295737e3b58664.
pendingCandidates=0, pendingDiscoveryRoots=0, typographyContinuationsPending=0, textBlocksDiscovered=175, textBlocksProcessed=175 and verificationFailures=0.
RTLX-LIMIT-001 recorded candidateBudgetReached=true and continuationQueued=true; final discovery queues and active cursors were zero.
The 289x2048 screenshot visually corroborates a rendered Persian/mixed Qwen conversation, but its resolution is insufficient for pixel-level font, caret or per-block assertions.

impact: Closes the broad 'no real 15.8.0 Qwen execution evidence' gap for this one captured page/state.
Does not falsify RTLX-TEXT-BLOCK-003 because this report records 175 text blocks, below the 512-descendant enumeration boundary.
Does not exercise the dynamic protection-cache transitions in RTLX-CACHE-001.
reproduction: Validate the JSON against failure-evidence.schema.json and recompute reportHash using canonicalizationVersion 1.0.0.
requiredTests: Repeat on a controlled same conversation before/after fixture with selected-element evidence and a region above the enumeration boundary.
Capture per-block visual assertions for headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, code and action icons.

rejectedHypotheses.hypothesis: Historical RTLX 15.7.3 Qwen/DeepSeek evidence proves RTLX 15.8.0 runtime behavior
rejectedHypotheses.basis: Version metadata explicitly differs; historical files were used only as symptom/regression context.
CandidateDiscoveryCursor exposes hasMore and the real 15.8.0 report recorded RTLX-LIMIT-001 with continuationQueued=true, activeCursors=0 and drained queues.

rejectedHypotheses.hypothesis: Bounded typography slicing starves after the first slice
rejectedHypotheses.basis: The repository 121-node continuation test passed and the real report had typographyContinuationsPending=0. The confirmed cache defect is dependency invalidation, not slice starvation.

rejectedHypotheses.hypothesis: Protective profile precedence follows JSON order
rejectedHypotheses.basis: profile-zone.ts applies category rank before profile order; validate:profiles and precedence tests passed.
FrameRuntime rejects HTML/BODY candidates; no source mutation path setting global document direction was found; security and smoke checks passed.

rejectedHypotheses.hypothesis: Wrapper proliferation is present in the supplied Qwen capture
rejectedHypotheses.basis: ownedWrappers=0 and wrapperLifecycle.current=0 in the valid 15.8.0 report.

rejectedHypotheses.hypothesis: selected-element evidence reused a stale element after location change
rejectedHypotheses.basis: The current report cleared the mismatch and emitted RTLX-SELECTION-101 plus reason RTLX-FEC-SELECTION-LOCATION-MISMATCH-CLEARED.

rejectedHypotheses.hypothesis: CSS Custom Highlight absence is a release defect
rejectedHypotheses.basis: architecture-v15.8.md explicitly marks it deferred and optional.

rejectedHypotheses.hypothesis: typographyScansCompleted must equal unique text blocks
rejectedHypotheses.basis: The active architecture describes a completion counter, not a unique-object counter; unique text-block counts are separately exposed.

boundaryReview.architecture: Preserved in the audit; no source or release changes were made.
boundaryReview.permissions: Generated manifests retain activeTab, alarms, scripting and storage plus optional HTTP/HTTPS host permissions; no mandatory host_permissions were added.
boundaryReview.privacy: Failure evidence schema 1.2.0 enforces false for page text, full URL, query, fragment, form values, cookies, localStorage, network capture, screenshot and automatic upload. The supplied real report satisfies these fields.
boundaryReview.directionTypography: Direction and typography remain separate source paths. No global html/body direction mutation was found. Dynamic typography cache invalidation is the confirmed exception requiring repair.
boundaryReview.ownershipRollback: Hybrid ownership, MutationJournal and rollback paths are present; controlled smoke rollback passed. Long-run strong-reference behavior remains insufficient_evidence.
boundaryReview.scheduling: Candidate discovery and typography work are bounded and requeued; discovery continuation is confirmed. Text-block enumeration is the unbounded-contract gap because its local 512 cap has no cursor.
boundaryReview.profiles: All bundled profiles/schema fixtures validate and protective category precedence is source/test confirmed.
boundaryReview.browserCompatibility: Manifest differences are generated correctly and static lint passes. Exact artifact execution remains blocked outside the supplied real Edge/Qwen installation report.
boundaryReview.determinism: Canonical report hash recomputation and browser artifact/source equivalence pass. The stale top release manifest blocks complete release-integrity certification.

releaseGates: New boundary tests for >512 text blocks and typography protection transitions
Clean regenerated top-level and nested SHA-256 manifests
Zero high/critical npm audit findings or an explicitly approved exception
Exact Chromium/Edge/Firefox/Firefox Android execution where environments are available
Controlled Qwen regression fixture and real Claude composer evidence before claims broader than the fixed code paths
